incident_reconstruction.impact_direction: Head-on collision from the front, likely from an oncoming vehicle
incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The vehicle was likely hit head-on by an oncoming vehicle, causing severe damage to the front end, including the bumper, bonnet, and headlights.
reconstruction_bullets: The damage pattern shows severe impact to the front of the vehicle, with multiple components damaged, including the front bumper, bonnet, and both headlights.
The claimant's story of being hit by an overspeeding vehicle from the wrong side is consistent with the damage pattern, which indicates a head-on collision.
The extent of the damage suggests a high-speed impact, which aligns with the claimant's description of the other vehicle being overspeeding.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle was being driven on the road when an oncoming vehicle approached from the opposite direction.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The oncoming vehicle collided head-on with the claimant's vehicle, causing severe damage to the front end.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The claimant's vehicle came to a stop, with significant damage to the front end, while the other vehicle may have stopped or fled the scene.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence from the damage pattern and claimant's story suggest a high-speed head-on collision, consistent with the claimant's account.

incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 80% | Story Match: Yes

decision_reasons: Fraud score of 92 exceeds the IRDAI Reject threshold of 70
Policy was expired at the time of incident (12 days after policy expiry on 2026-05-20), making the claim ineligible
Garage estimate underdeclaration and physics mismatch indicators suggest potential fraud
Symmetrical Damage Anomaly and Plate mismatch indicators further support the rejection decision
reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment revealed a severe overall severity with a total repair estimate between ₹118,800 and ₹206,200
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence reported a high fraud score of 92 with indicators including Symmetrical Damage Anomaly, Physics Mismatch, and Garage estimate underdeclaration
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction confirmed the collision type as front impact with a confidence of 80
Step 4: Context & Policy verification revealed the policy had expired 12 days prior to the incident, rendering the claim ineligible
Step 5: Historical Precedents did not provide a direct match, but the high fraud score and policy expiry support the rejection decision
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to the rejection decision
Step 7: Final Decision is to reject the claim based on the high fraud score, policy expiry, and other indicators
